notes: The townsfolk of Salmonberry, 1963-64. bond_lines surface as the bond deepens (index 0 at bond>=2, 1 at >=4, 2 at >=6). The grandmother (gran) is the spine and the true-ending gate.

npcs.name: Vovo
npcs.role: your grandmother · the house at the river mouth
npcs.intro: She meets the bus with a coat that is not yours and is exactly your size.
bond_lines: She hums while she works. You do not know the words. They are not English.
She teaches you the song about the boat that goes out and the boat that comes back. There are more verses than she sings.
She sings you all of it, the last verse too, the one for the boat that does not come back. Now you know it. Now it is yours to carry.

npcs.name: Del
npcs.role: harbormaster · fifty years on the bar
npcs.intro: He watches you look at the water like he is deciding something.
bond_lines: He shows you how to read the bar — where it stands up, where it lies down.
He lets you take the skiff out past the jetty on a slack tide. Alone.
He tells you what he saw the morning the boat did not come back in. He has told no one.

npcs.name: Iris
npcs.role: librarian · the one warm room on Main
npcs.intro: She stamps your card before you finish asking for one.
bond_lines: She keeps the tide tables and the bird books behind the desk, for the ones who ask.
She lends you the county history, the real one, not the one they printed.
She tells you the library is the town's memory and someone has to be the one who keeps it. She is looking at you when she says it.

npcs.name: Manny
npcs.intro: He puts you on the line without asking if you have done it before.
bond_lines: He shows you the trick of the knife so your hands stop bleeding by the end of the shift.
He tells you the cannery has maybe three seasons left and to learn something else.
He says the town is the cannery and when it goes the town goes, and he is not angry about it, which is worse.

npcs.name: Ruth
npcs.intro: She sets down a plate you did not order and will not let you pay for.
bond_lines: She knows everyone's order and everyone's business and keeps both.
She tells you who to trust and who to only be kind to.
She says the cafe is where the town says the things it cannot say at home, and she is the one who holds them.

npcs.name: Boyd
npcs.role: a kid your age · knows the woods
npcs.intro: He is up a spruce before he says hello.
bond_lines: He shows you the elk trail and the good salmonberry patch and swears you to secrecy.
He takes you to the place on the bluff where you can see the whole coast at once.
He tells you he is going to leave the first chance he gets, and asks if you will too, and means it as the biggest question there is.

npcs.name: Estelle
npcs.role: the gray house past the church
npcs.intro: She does not come to the door but the curtain moves.
bond_lines: She lets you bring in her wood. She does not say much.
She keeps a light on at the window that faces the bar, every night, still.
She says his name to you, once, the son who took the boat out. She has not said it aloud in a year.

npcs.name: Tuck
npcs.role: the store · knows the price of everything
npcs.intro: He sizes you up like inventory.
bond_lines: He fronts you a nickel's credit and remembers it exactly.
He tells you the state men were in, measuring the highway, and what it will do to Main.
He shows you the ledger going back forty years — every family, what they bought, what they owed. The town, in his hand.
